# Smart City Series: Augmented Reality/Virtual Reality (AR/VR)

All visualization techniques we used in the past -- sketches, scaled models, 3D renderings, etc. are substitutions for the built reality. They are tools that aid planners and designers to communicate to stakeholders what the future holds. AR/VR is the next substitution in line. In this issue, let’s take a look at the roles that AR/VR play in smart city development and public engagement. May the glimpse of the latest AR/VR technologies give you insights on the planning/design work at hand!

### America’s Mayors Gather to Experience The Future of City Planning Through VR

Publisher: HTC VIVE

### Community-based Urban Design in the Virtual Reality Dome

Publisher: Pixel Legend

Having taken a look at the applications of AR/VR, let’s get to know about HoloLens--one of the examples on application of the latest AR/VR technologies from the following videos.

### Build a Whole City in Your Mind and We Augment It to Your Eyes - A City Planning App with HoloLens

Publisher: HoloPundits

### HoloLens City Model Viewer | The Future of Urban Planning!

Publisher: Sean Ong

The next two videos will give us a glimpse of the technical processes that may involve, such that we can gain some basic ideas when managing relevant processes/projects.

### Virtual Reality for Urban Planning

Publisher: Planetizen Courses

### CityEngine and ArcGIS 360 VR

Publisher: ESRI Australia

# Smart Cities

AR / VR / Projection / Web

![](https://theendless.co/wp-content/uploads/2020/12/pgim-vignette-2.jpg)
![](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns=%22http://www.w3.org/2000/svg%22%20viewBox=%220%200%20210%20140%22%3E%3C/svg%3E)

WHAT ROLES DO AR/VR PLAY IN THE CITY OF THE FUTURE?

5G, IoT sensors, smartphones, and a renewed focus on culture have created a prime opportunity for Augmented and Virtual Reality to impact urban environments. These technologies serve as interfaces to visualize and interact with smart cities' unique spatial datasets, so public and private stakeholders can imagine, analyze, and actualize the cities of our future. AR/VR provide novel opportunities in Urban Planning, Wayfinding, Data Visualization, Tourism, Education, Art, and Infrastructure and Maintenance.

##### INDUSTRIES

##### Benefits

### PGIM

**INDUSTRY:** FINANCIAL SERVICES

**REQUEST:** PGIM, the investment arm of Prudential Financial, commissioned The Endless Co. with building an Augmented Reality experience to exhibit futuristic investment areas on the floor of the Milken Institute Global Conference.

**SMART CITY PRODUCT:** The tabletop AR module visualizes five areas that PGIM imagines will define the cities of the future: warehouse automation, lithium mining for solar energy, seaports in emerging markets, urban real estate development, and drone delivery systems.

AR scenes are modeled and animated with AI scripts to accurately represent the processes at an Amazon warehouse, Chilean mine, or Thai seaport.

### 

![](https://theendless.co/wp-content/uploads/2020/12/PGIM_Warehouse_0408_WIP_3-1024x550-1.jpg)
![](https://theendless.co/wp-content/uploads/2020/12/screen-16.51.40-1024x576-1.png)
![](https://theendless.co/wp-content/uploads/2020/12/vlcsnap-2019-06-25-12h30m04s744_ALPHA-1024x638-1.png)
![](https://theendless.co/wp-content/uploads/2020/12/PGIM_Seaport_0408_WIP_4-1024x733-1.jpg)
![](https://theendless.co/wp-content/uploads/2020/12/PGIM_Drone_Delivery_0409_WIP_1-1024x550-1.jpg)

### VERIZON

**INDUSTRY:** GOVERNMENT + ARCHITECTURE

**REQUEST:** Superbright, Verizon, and Vrai Pictures partnered with The Endless Co. to build an Augmented Reality experience that engages community members on Frank Gerhy’s Los Angeles River revitalization project.

**SMART CITY PRODUCT:** We designed the 3D assets for an Urban Planning AR module that invites neighbors into the major design decisions that will impact their home for years to come.

### 

![](https://theendless.co/wp-content/uploads/2020/12/LA-RIVER_EDITED-1024x667-1.jpg)
![](https://theendless.co/wp-content/uploads/2020/12/IMG_0042-1024x768-1.png)
![](https://theendless.co/wp-content/uploads/2020/12/LA-Height-Topo-Material-Tests-WOOD-1024x576-1.jpg)
![](https://theendless.co/wp-content/uploads/2020/12/IMG_0040-1024x768-1.png)
![](https://theendless.co/wp-content/uploads/2020/12/914A3933-1024x683-1.jpg)
![](https://theendless.co/wp-content/uploads/2020/12/IMG_0038-1024x768-1.png)
![](https://theendless.co/wp-content/uploads/2020/12/PaperCityPoster-HIGH-RES-1024x576-1.png)

### SUPERCELL

**INDUSTRY:** ART / GAMING

**REQUEST:** SUPERCELL, makers of the celebrated mobile game “Clash of Clans,” worked with The Endless Co. to explore the future of urban art installations.

**SMART CITY PRODUCT:** The project explores the role of both physical and digital street art in the smart cities of our future. Our architectural visualization team built a photorealistic virtual neighborhood inspired by graffiti epicenters like South Side Chicago and Brooklyn. We then commissioned physical wall-scale graffiti artworks in cities like New York, San Francisco, and Los Angeles and captured and composited into them into the virtual city environment.

[*Read the full case study here*.](https://theendless.co/work/supercell/)

![]()
![](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns=%22http://www.w3.org/2000/svg%22%20viewBox=%220%200%20210%20140%22%3E%3C/svg%3E)

### WESTERN DIGITAL

**INDUSTRY:** INFORMATION TECHNOLOGY

**REQUEST:** Western Digital, a global data storage provider, envisions a data-driven society in the year 2049. Autonomous vehicles fill city streets, stitched together in lightning fast webs powered by 7G networks. Together with agency partners Condé Nast Experiential, The Endless Co. pioneered a self-driving car experience to immerse riders in Western Digital’s imagined futuristic city.

**SMART CITY PRODUCT:** Our visual effects team created a cinematic-quality city environment — from the backseat, the rider watches as the car traverses the streets of the 2049 metropolis. Additional head’s up displays on the windows include all the car’s modules, navigation menus, and information data. Western Digital data storage technology is present at every turn, showcasing the data infrastructure needed to operate both the autonomous vehicle and the operations of the larger 7G-connected city.

[*Read the full case study here*.](https://theendless.co/work/western-digital/)

**Augmented Reality (AR) & Virtual Reality (VR) in Data Visualization**

The rapid evolution of technology has transformed how we interact with data. From traditional spreadsheets and graphs to sophisticated dashboards and AI-driven insights, data visualization has come a long way. However, the next big leap in data visualization is being driven by **Augmented Reality (AR) and Virtual Reality (VR)**. These immersive technologies are reshaping the way professionals analyze, interpret, and interact with complex datasets.

In India, where digital transformation is occurring at an unprecedented pace, AR and VR are making inroads across multiple sectors, including **healthcare, finance, education, and smart cities**. With Hyderabad emerging as a major **tech hub**, known for its IT and data science innovation, it is not surprising that AR and VR applications in data visualization are gaining traction. The city’s thriving ecosystem, bolstered by **tech giants, startups, and academic institutions**, makes it an ideal location to explore **data science advancements**.

## Get Aditya Tripathi’s stories in your inbox

Join Medium for free to get updates from this writer.

For professionals and students looking to stay ahead in this technological revolution, enrolling in a **data science course Hyderabad** is a great way to gain expertise in cutting-edge data visualization techniques, including AR and VR integrations.

# Understanding AR & VR in Data Visualization

Before diving into applications and use cases, it’s essential to differentiate between AR and VR:

* **Augmented Reality (AR):** Enhances real-world environments by overlaying digital content, such as data, images, and graphs, onto the physical surroundings using devices like smartphones, tablets, or AR glasses.
* **Virtual Reality (VR):** Creates an entirely simulated digital environment where users can immerse themselves and interact with 3D visualized data using VR headsets.

Both AR and VR bring a **new dimension to data visualization**, enabling users to **see, explore, and manipulate data** beyond the limitations of 2D screens.

# The Role of AR & VR in Data Visualization

Traditional data visualization relies on charts, graphs, and dashboards, which can be limiting when analyzing massive datasets. AR and VR introduce an **interactive, 3D approach** to data interpretation. Here’s how they transform data visualization:

## 1. Enhanced Data Interaction

* With AR and VR, users can **physically interact with data models** by zooming, rotating, or slicing through different layers of information.
* Analysts can **walk through complex datasets** in a VR environment, making it easier to understand intricate patterns.
* Real-time AR overlays allow professionals to visualize data on **real-world objects** such as machinery, buildings, or medical scans.

## 2. Better Pattern Recognition

* Traditional 2D graphs may fail to capture intricate relationships within big data.
* AR & VR help users to **detect trends, anomalies, and correlations** more intuitively by using **spatial visualization**.
* In finance, for example, AR dashboards can provide **real-time stock market trends** in a visually compelling format.

## 3. Collaboration & Decision-Making

* VR allows teams in **different locations to collaborate** in a virtual space, analyzing the same datasets in a **shared immersive environment**.
* AR enables decision-makers to **visualize business data** on mobile devices or wearables, facilitating quicker insights.
* Engineers and architects can **use AR overlays** to analyze city planning and infrastructure data in real time.

## 4. Real-Time Data Monitoring

* AR-enabled **smart glasses** allow factory workers and medical professionals to monitor live data feeds **without needing a screen**.
* VR-based simulations can provide **real-time analytics dashboards** for industries like logistics, aviation, and healthcare.

# Applications of AR & VR in Data Visualization Across Industries

## 1. Healthcare & Medical Data

* AR and VR help **doctors visualize patient data**, such as MRI and CT scans, in 3D.
* Medical researchers can **explore genomic data** using immersive VR models.
* AR-assisted surgeries allow surgeons to overlay real-time data on patients during procedures.

## 2. Financial Analytics

* Traders and financial analysts use **VR-powered stock market visualization** to interact with economic trends.
* AR enables real-time visualization of investment portfolios and risk analysis.
* AI-driven VR tools can create **predictive models** for better financial forecasting.

## 3. Education & Learning

* Universities and **data science course Hyderabad** programs are incorporating AR & VR into their curriculum to teach complex concepts interactively.
* AR-based mobile apps allow students to explore data science models in real-world environments.
* VR classrooms enable **immersive learning experiences** for coding, statistics, and data modeling.

## 4. Smart Cities & Urban Planning

* Governments are using AR overlays to visualize **traffic, population density, and environmental data**.
* VR simulations assist urban planners in **designing infrastructure projects** with real-time data.
* AR dashboards enable citizens to engage with city-wide data, promoting transparency.

## 5. Business Intelligence & Marketing

* AR-driven dashboards allow businesses to **visualize sales performance** and customer insights dynamically.
* VR-powered customer analytics help brands understand consumer behavior **in a 3D space**.
* AR-based marketing campaigns use interactive data overlays for **personalized customer experiences**.

# AR & VR Tools for Data Visualization

Several **cutting-edge tools** are being developed to enable AR and VR-based data visualization. Some popular ones include:

* **Tableau VR:** A VR-enabled version of the popular Tableau platform for immersive data exploration.
* **Google ARCore & Apple ARKit:** Development platforms for creating AR-based visualization applications.
* **Unity & Unreal Engine:** Used to build interactive VR simulations for data-driven insights.
* **Microsoft HoloLens:** An AR headset that integrates real-world data overlays for various industries.
* **IBM Immersive Insights:** AI-powered VR data visualization for business intelligence.

# Future of AR & VR in Data Visualization

The future of **data visualization in India** is set to be revolutionized by **AR and VR innovations**. As industries increasingly adopt **data-driven decision-making**, immersive visualization will become the norm. Key trends to watch include:

* **AI-Integrated AR & VR Analytics:** AI-powered visualization tools will enhance AR and VR experiences, making data exploration more intuitive.
* **5G-Powered AR & VR Applications:** Faster data processing speeds will enable real-time, **high-quality data visualization** in immersive environments.
* **Growth of AR & VR in Education:** Data science courses, including **data science course Hyderabad**, will integrate AR & VR learning modules to improve student engagement.
* **Expansion in IoT & Smart Devices:** AR dashboards linked to IoT devices will enable **real-time business intelligence insights**.

# Conclusion

AR and VR are no longer futuristic concepts; they are **transforming the way we visualize and interact with data**. From finance and healthcare to education and smart cities, these technologies offer **immersive and dynamic insights** that go beyond traditional methods.

Hyderabad, as a growing **data science and technology hub**, is at the forefront of this revolution. Whether you are a student, professional, or entrepreneur, investing in **AR & VR-based data visualization skills** will set you apart in today’s competitive job market.

For those looking to master these cutting-edge techniques, enrolling in a [**data science course Hyderabad**](https://bostoninstituteofanalytics.org/india/hyderabad/hitec-city/school-of-technology-ai/data-science-and-artificial-intelligence/) is the perfect starting point. It provides hands-on exposure to **AR & VR data visualization**, helping individuals stay ahead in the rapidly evolving field of **data science and analytics**.

# Industry Thought Leadership

### AR and VR Use Cases in Smart Cities

![](images/thought-leadership/authors/Rohit Sethi.jpg)

More cities are going ‘Smart’ with the intent to leverage the significant benefits that new technological advancements promise to offer towards transforming the way we live, work and recreate in the years to come, by enhancing our interaction with data and technology as never before. These technologies can also play a strong role in addressing issues that are more adversely impacting the urban areas globally. Increasing population density in the urban cities leads to growing concerns around safety, security and holistic wellbeing of individuals, and is becoming a top priority in the policymaker’s agenda. Smart Cities are better geared towards addressing these problems through a combination of data and technology, ensuring a positive impact on the local community and the overall environment. Given the increased availability of affordable smartphones and other IoT devices as well as reliable broadband connectivity, Augmented Reality (AR) and Virtual Reality (VR) have emerged as key technologies that would enable unlocking of new opportunities in the vibrant Smart City ecosystem.

AR, where data interacts with the real environment, enables over-laying enhancements such as graphs, sound, text and effects to improve user’s real-world experiences. While AR interacts with the real environment, VR is a simulated experience that can be similar or completely different from the real world. The power of VR lies in allowing a user to experience and interact in an artificial environment as if it were real. The combination of Smart devices and infrastructure with advanced AR/VR systems will allow users living in or even visiting Smart Cities, to make more optimal choices towards an enhanced standard of living/ experience.

Governmental entities, enterprises and individuals can leverage AR and VR to carry out their functions more efficiently and with an improved user experience. Most advanced uses cases built upon these technologies in the Smart City ecosystem include the following:

**Urban planning:** Cities are now having higher constraints than ever before in terms of space, mobility, privacy, security and sustainability. Supported by VR technology, Smart City planners and developers can effectively assess whether urban planning models would fit the existing infrastructure, and have a better understanding of what to build and where. Additionally, VR could also involve stakeholders such as residents or investors in the decision-making process, giving them an opportunity to provide feedback as to how cities could be better transformed and developed. For example, in 2016, MIT collaborated with the City of Hamburg to model potential locations for refugee accommodations. Using optically-tagged LEGO bricks, simulation algorithms, and AR to model potential locations for refugee accommodation, local leaders and community members participated in this process, identifying 160 potential locations and finally building 10, successfully avoiding social opposition that these kinds of projects typically entail.

**Emergency management & policing:** Equipment and gadgets enhanced with AR technology can provide relevant information on the environment, improving the decision-making of police and emergency teams in critical situations. For example, AR could allow having access to 3D models of locations where an emergency is occurring, or provide information of previous or simultaneous events in real time. In addition, AR combined with object recognition, particularly face recognition, can help authorities to identify suspicious behavior and prevent crimes in their early stages. As futuristic as this may seem, Beijing-based AR company Xloong has created Smart glasses already used by the Chinese police, giving them access to real time facial, identification card and vehicle plate information that are linked with a national database. In addition, VR can immerse police officers into virtual training experiences, placing them into threat situations in a secure simulated environment. Exposing them to real life high-stakes situations and allowing them to deal with these in a controlled environment enhances officer effectiveness during actual crises. Without VR, providing such real life trainings would be both expensive and time-consuming.

**Education & training:** VR is ideally positioned to support enterprises and educational institutions in providing innovative and effective learning, overcoming the typical space and time constraints of urban cities as well as the potential risks associated with learning directly from real life situations. With the capacity to recreate real life experiences with a high level of detail, AR and VR could revolutionize training for students and professionals in many fields, for example, performance support in critical positions such as medical surgery or heavy machinery operation. Additionally, AR and VR will anyways enhance traditional learning with real time updated interactive content and exercises across the board.

**Culture & tourism:** AR can also transform the experience of visitors to a Smart City. Solutions such as AR real time translators or AR-enhanced visits to key tourist attractions providing additional historical/ cultural information to landmarks, can be leveraged by local businesses to improve customer experience and promote their services. In France, local entrepreneurs supported by the French Ministry of Culture and Communication have developed Culture Click, a mobile app that uses AR to discover geolocated works of art in high definition, to access French museums’ information and to discover cultural events across cities.

**Navigation:** Navigation apps are already a tool of prime importance for urban commuters, recommending optimized routes and real time information about traffic events. However, current solutions are frequently unclear and inconvenient especially for drivers. Creating an AR layer for navigation that adds relevant content on top of the image captured by the camera of a smartphone, can significantly improve user experience and overall journey safety. In August 2019, Google Maps launched an AR mode for iOS and Android with new capabilities to its popular Navigation app.

**Medical services:** In addition to constantly enhancing the standard of living, Smart Cities also focus on improving individual mental health and overall well-being. AR applied to the healthcare sector can improve therapeutic methods as well as reduce customer discomfort. The technology can be used to evaluate the results of cosmetic surgery such as facial reconstruction, giving both the patient and the doctor the ability to see the results before the operation. Moreover, VR’s ability to reproduce real life environments can be quite helpful in the treatment of mental health illnesses such as Post Traumatic Stress Disorder.

While it is evident that significant value can be created by deployment of the above indicated use cases, a collaborative and cohesive effort is required across multiple stakeholders at the city level including governmental entities, corporates and local user communities, in order to ensure effective implementation of the use cases. Additionally, while the technology to enable most of the above is already available, it is imperative that each city prioritizes use cases specific to its unique context and requirement, to optimize on the cost of as well as on the value realized from such deployment. Adequate levels of safety and privacy/ security must also be critical considerations into the strategy and implementation for AR/VR use cases. As with any other connected technology, AR is vulnerable to security threats and unauthorized access by hacker attacks and malware. These attacks can result in a denial of service or overlay of wrong information, potentially even leading to severe, catastrophic consequences. Devising ways to preserve personal privacy with mass propagation of AR will also be a challenge required to be addressed. Successful overcoming the above challenges on an ongoing basis, as more and more innovative/ encompassing use cases emerge, Smart Cities would be able to realize the immense value that AR/VR technologies can unlock for everyone.

# AR, VR, Gamification: Cutting-edge technologies applied in Smart Cities

## AR, VR, Gamification: Cutting-edge technologies applied in Smart Cities

Friday, 23rd of November of 2018

The recent advancement in smart cities and IoT technologies increased the need in the development of cutting-edge Virtual Reality (VR) and Augmented Reality (AR) applications. Although these technologies are parallel, they are not similar. VR technology is used when a projection of the real world is generated by the computer, and includes the user interaction in the designed environment. Depending on the degree of immersion, the user can interact with objects from the simulated world, or be just a simple spectator and experiment realistically the visual and auditory elements. Furthermore, the VR gives users the access to specific experiences that we would be difficult to access in reality.

![](http://www.citisim.org/wp-content/uploads/2019/07/2018-11-22_Imagen_2.png)

Instead of VR, the AR technology allow users to see or perceive a virtual world generated by the computer, integrated into reality. The “real world” is the environment that the user can be an observer, and can “see” through his own senses. The virtual world is defined as a generated, stored and calculated environment using a processor. An AR monitoring system records virtual elements within the real world in order to integrate virtual and real-world information so that the observer can use them.

![](http://www.citisim.org/wp-content/uploads/2019/07/2018-11-22_Imagen_1.png)

Through the above-mentioned technologies, a large number of applications can be adapted and integrated in the development of Smart Cities digital twins. It represents an ideal environment for deploying VR and AR solutions for designing and simulating different layers. One of the layers can be represented by the 3D urban planning and modelling. The significant roles of AR and VR technologies are targeting real-time simulations and visualization requirements used in a smart city.

The gaming engines have become flexible tools for developing VR applications due to its ability to meet the requirements of a VR-performing applications such us 3D modelling of smart cities. The game engines contain a list of features including high-quality rendering capability, physical simulations, real-time lighting, code editing, and real-time performance generators / editors. There are currently a few high-performance engines available, and the most preferred solution is Unity3D. This engine has been identified as the preference of independent developers and gaming enthusiasts, due to a combination of capabilities and accessibility.

![](http://www.citisim.org/wp-content/uploads/2019/07/2018-11-22_Imagen_3.png)

A successful VR application of 3D urban design using Unity 3D should provide accurate visualization that is realistic and validated for further designs and also, should convince audience about its viability. This can contribute to the concept of strategic planning of smart cities, and motivate public authorities and private companies to participate in policy processes and initiatives for a sustainable urban growth.

![](http://www.citisim.org/wp-content/uploads/2019/07/2018-11-22_Imagen_4.png)
![](http://www.citisim.org/wp-content/uploads/2019/07/2018-11-22_Imagen_5.png)
![](http://www.citisim.org/wp-content/uploads/2019/07/2018-11-22_Imagen_6.png)
![](http://www.citisim.org/wp-content/uploads/2019/07/2018-11-22_Imagen_7.png)

The game engine Unity3D saves time and avoids a great number of issues in writing code, offers productivity and workflow features. It contributes in the development of planning houses and levels, constructions and streets, can support digital services, use open data and integrate different sensors in order to interact with the environment.

In conclusion, the using of VR, AR and game engines in designing smart cities can produce high-quality digital infrastructure capable to assess and create ideas in real time, and within a 3D space, during the design and planning phase. Also, can provide effective communication among the communities, that lead to a strategic and sustainable planning. Nevertheless, can contribute in saving a significant amount of time, funds and resources.

**Author:**[Carmen Catalina Rusu](https://www.linkedin.com/in/carmen-rusu-08571026)  
**Company:**[ALTFACTOR, Galati – Romania](https://altfactor.ro/)

enriched_points.expanded_content: AR and VR technologies transform urban planning by providing immersive environments for stakeholders, enhancing communication through realistic simulations of future city developments.
Geospatial big data enables cities to analyze spatial patterns and trends, improving decision-making and resource allocation to enhance urban living and sustainability.
Predictive analytics utilizes historical data to forecast future urban trends, helping city planners anticipate needs and optimize infrastructure development for better services.
